title: Platinum Artifact Collection And Analysis Dashboard
strapline: A dashboard-first intake and decision cockpit for current games and future additions: gather source artifacts, analyze them, turn them into a Platinum-focused instantiation plan, and keep conformance work grounded in durable evidence.
currentGoal: Make artifact collection and analysis the front door for every game on Platinum, with Aurora and Galaxy Guardians visible today and Windigo Invaders entering through a measured, source-first intake path.
dashboardRead: This page should be the primary place where new game materials are added, assessed, and turned into explicit build decisions. It should stay compact at the top level, let us click into detail by game, and keep showing what source families are still missing before design outruns evidence.

phaseDefinitions.label: Artifact Collection
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Accession real source materials first: gameplay video, manuals, score tables, stills, cabinet art, and written reference pages.

phaseDefinitions.label: Analysis
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Turn sources into reviewable windows and evidence: contact sheets, waveforms, semantic notes, timing slices, and confidence gaps.

phaseDefinitions.label: Platinum Instantiation Plan
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Convert evidence into an explicit game plan that separates Platinum-owned host work from game-owned rules, timing, audio, and identity.

phaseDefinitions.label: Implementation
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Build only the smallest evidence-backed slice needed to prove the game on Platinum.

phaseDefinitions.label: Conformance
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Measure the game against its source lineage with runtime artifacts, personas, and visible review surfaces.

phaseDefinitions.label: Release Review
phaseDefinitions.purpose: Decide whether the game is ready for public claims, beta review, or continued private iteration.

artifactFamilies.label: Gameplay video
artifactFamilies.detail: Longplay, attract, operator, and targeted scenario windows with clear provenance and usable audio.

artifactFamilies.label: Manuals and rule documents
artifactFamilies.detail: Operator sheets, score tables, cabinet docs, and written descriptions of mechanics and progression.

artifactFamilies.label: Still-image references
artifactFamilies.detail: Cabinet, bezel, title, score-table, sprite-family, cannon/ship, bunker, and HUD references.

artifactFamilies.label: Audio references
artifactFamilies.detail: Isolated or cleaner windows for cue identity, tempo, density, and game-event timing.

artifactFamilies.label: Semantic timing windows
artifactFamilies.detail: Event-labeled clips for start, fire, hit, descent, failure, transition, and results moments.

artifactFamilies.label: Runtime correspondence captures
artifactFamilies.detail: Persona-driven or manual recordings from Platinum that can be reviewed against the reference set.

globalDocs.label: Classic Arcade Ingestion Framework
globalDocs.detail: Canonical source-first intake process for future games.

globalDocs.label: Game Conformance Catalog
globalDocs.detail: Cross-game maintained index for enemies, audio, stages, and personas.

globalDocs.label: Reference Media Inventory
globalDocs.detail: Shared media inventory and source-of-truth reference ledger.

globalDocs.label: Platinum Audio Conformance Framework
globalDocs.detail: Generic audio intake, scene review, and live-mix framework for any future game.

globalHunts: Find manual and score-table sources early so scoring, bonus, and staging do not remain implied.
Prefer at least one longplay plus one cleaner short-window video per game line.
Promote audio windows that match real gameplay scenes, not only isolated cue existence.
Keep every game's plan explicit about what Platinum owns versus what the game owns.
If a missing artifact family repeatedly blocks conformance, add it to the standing intake requirement set.

platformExtensions.title: Generic preserved-source package contract
platformExtensions.detail: Every new game should enter through the same manifest, checksum, preview-artifact, and README pattern used here for Windigo Invaders.

platformExtensions.title: Generic audio scene and live-mix review
platformExtensions.detail: The new audio scene/live-mix analyzers should remain game-configured so a third game can use them without bespoke platform rewrites.

platformExtensions.title: Persona capture and review rail
platformExtensions.detail: Future games should inherit the same persona-driven capture, review, and replay discipline once their rules are mature enough.

games.title: Aurora Galactica
games.themeRead: The most mature current game. Intake and conformance should stay visible because future games should inherit the process, not just the runtime.

phaseStatus.summary: Reference and extracted media families are established.
Challenge, sprite, motion, and artifact scoring are already active.
Measured quality gaps are explicit and documented.
Still improving challenge-stage and motion fidelity.
Public family live, `/dev` remains the review lane.

headlineScores.label: Challenge-stage conformance
headlineScores.detail: Still the clearest weak user-facing area.

games.decisionRead: Aurora should continue using this dashboard as the visible intake and quality cockpit, but the other machine can keep driving the deeper challenge-stage work while this repo retains the authoritative status surface.

hostedLinks.label: Aurora conformance dashboard
hostedLinks.detail: Direct Aurora conformance view on the current lane.

hostedLinks.label: Aurora application guide
hostedLinks.detail: Aurora-specific game, audio, stage, and persona guide.

items.label: Challenge-stage conformance
items.detail: Current strict challenge-stage scoring and explanation.

items.label: Aurora sprite motion correspondence
items.detail: Current motion correspondence scorer for visible alien cadence.

planTracks.title: Keep Aurora as the process template
planTracks.goal: Future games should inherit Aurora's measurement discipline without inheriting Aurora-specific truth.
deliverables: Keep hosted docs aligned with current conformance artifacts
Preserve audio and motion harnesses as generic platform contracts where possible

nextArtifacts: Stronger motion-reference windows for flap cadence and challenge novelty
Better boss and explosion lifecycle visual references

games.title: Galaxy Guardians
games.lineage: Galaxian-style second-game ingestion proof on Platinum
games.themeRead: The current visible second-game proof. It is strong enough to deserve deeper artifact discipline, but still weak enough that the dashboard should keep missing evidence and risk areas visible.

phaseStatus.summary: Multiple Galaxian-family sources and extracted windows already exist.
Audio, movement, long-surface, and visual identity artifacts are now substantial.
Top-ten and audio sprint plans exist, but deeper play quality still needs work.
Playable preview on Platinum with game-owned identity.
Reference and playtest reads are honest and still below public-release confidence.
Useful as a review artifact, not yet a mature standalone public promise.

headlineScores.label: Public readiness
headlineScores.detail: Still too low for a strong public promise.

headlineScores.label: Audio reference comparison
headlineScores.detail: Good coverage, still weak direct acoustic proxy fit.

headlineScores.label: Audio live mix
headlineScores.detail: Live mix is now strong; source-faithful match is the remaining gap.

games.decisionRead: Guardians should use this dashboard as the main cross-artifact intake and planning cockpit while deeper runtime quality work continues. The current best return remains midrun fairness plus serious audio conformance work, backed by the new generic audio framework.

hostedLinks.label: Guardians project guide entry points
hostedLinks.detail: Resume note, opening baseline, long-surface review, and first-class plan.

items.label: Guardians safe-lane and persona subplan

planTracks.title: Audio authenticity sprint
planTracks.goal: Make gameplay sound more source-faithful, not just technically busy.
deliverables: Scene review and live-mix acceptance gates
Shot, dive, hit, and loss family rebuilds against reference windows

planTracks.title: Deeper-play fairness
planTracks.goal: Improve stage-five-and-beyond survivability without restoring fake-safe lanes.

nextArtifacts: Cleaner isolated Galaxian cabinet audio windows
Direct alien pulse and flap reference windows
Additional score-popup and hit-value reference windows

games.title: Windigo Invaders
games.lineage: Space Invaders-derived future Platinum game, themed eventually around the Windigo legend
games.themeRead: This should enter through evidence first. The goal is not to rush a derivative shell, but to build the strongest artifact package and Platinum instantiation plan before design decisions harden.

phaseStatus.summary: First gameplay source accessioned and preserved.
First contact sheet and waveform generated.
Need a Platinum-focused instantiation plan from the source evidence.
Do not build runtime slices before the initial evidence package is strong enough.
Scorers should come after the first explicit plan and runtime slice.
No public claim yet; this is still in intake.

headlineScores.label: Current phase
headlineScores.detail: The game is intentionally still pre-runtime.

headlineScores.label: Seed source count
headlineScores.detail: One preserved gameplay source is now in-repo.

headlineScores.label: First-pass previews
headlineScores.detail: Contact sheet plus audio waveform generated.

games.decisionRead: The next best spend is on artifact breadth, not code. We should use this dashboard to decide what missing source families matter most before we commit to game-specific runtime assumptions.

planTracks.goal: Separate what Platinum will host from what Windigo Invaders must own as game truth.

deliverables: Still-image references for title, bunkers, cannon, and invader rows
